background: Consider a standby system with two identical valves each with constant failure rate $\lambda=10^{-5}$ failures/hours. The probability $\mathrm{p}_{\mathrm{s}}$ that the switch $S$ will activate the standby valve is 0.995.

task.query: What is the reliability of the system at time $\mathrm{t}=8760 \mathrm{~h}$ (or one year)?
task.answer: Here $p_s=0.995$, from Eq. (4.48) the reliability that the system will survive one year is, $$\begin{aligned} R(8760) & =\left(1+p_s \lambda t\right) e^{-\lambda t} \\ & =\left(1+(0.995)\left(10^{-5}\right) 8760\right) e^{-\left(10^{-5}\right) 8760} \\ & =0.9960 \end{aligned}$$

task.query: What is the system MTTF (Mean Time To Failure) for the identical valves scenario?
task.answer: The system MTTF is $$\begin{aligned} M T T F & =\frac{1+p_s}{\lambda} \\ & =\frac{1+0.995}{10^{-5}}=199,500 \ \text { hours } \end{aligned}$$

task.query: What is the reliability of the system when the components are non-identical, with component 1 (active unit) and component 2 (standby unit) having constant failure rates $\lambda_1$ and $\lambda_2$, respectively, and $p_s$ being the probability of successful switching?
task.answer: From Eq. (4.45), the reliability is $$\begin{aligned} R(t) & =e^{-\lambda_1 t}+p_s \int_0^t \lambda_1 e^{-\lambda_1 \ \tau} e^{-\lambda_2(t-\ \tau)} d \ \tau \\ & =e^{-\lambda_1 t}+\frac{p_s \lambda_1}{\lambda_1-\lambda_2} e^{-\lambda_2 t}-\frac{p_s \lambda_1}{\lambda_1-\lambda_2} e^{-\lambda_1 t} \end{aligned}$$

task.query: What is the system MTTF (Mean Time To Failure) for the non-identical components scenario?
task.answer: The system MTTF is given by $$\begin{aligned} M T T F & =\int_0^{\infty} R(t) d t=\int_0^{\infty}\left(e^{-\lambda t}+\frac{p_s \lambda_1}{\lambda_1-\lambda_2} e^{-\lambda_2 t}-\frac{p_s \lambda_1}{\lambda_1-\lambda_2} e^{-\lambda_1 t}\right) d t \\ & =\frac{1}{\lambda_1}+\frac{p_s}{\lambda_2} \end{aligned}$$
